notes: Pure deterministic result and node helpers.
Stdout/stderr output is profile-mapped, but the module surface is available on every supported target.
Pure deterministic bytes, base64, and UTF-8 helpers.
Pure deterministic integer helpers, comparison, formatting, and parsing.
Pure deterministic integer arithmetic helpers; division and modulo are deferred until the numeric error/result contract is settled.
Metadata exports are broadly available; openDefault/openUrl remain host-only and are not browser baseline capabilities.
Native host process control exists; browser targets do not expose process lifecycle.
Native host filesystem exists; browser targets require explicit remote or host-side adapters.
Time access exists but target policy differs and browser behavior must remain explicit and deterministic.
Production-safe diagnostics only: mode, stderr logging, and assertions. Capture, replay, artifacts, and tracing belong to debug/profile runtimes, not the production stdlib surface.
